Do not reintroduce this already-fixed issue: The hero headline has too many competing accent moves at once: the pink word, the pink end bracket, and the detached leading plus. Instead of one clean punch, the eye bounces between ornaments. (brand rule: no more than one emphasis color per headline)

hits.snippet: Pursue this winning direction (its fixes verifiably stuck): Turn the opening into a true type-led TikTok poster by rebuilding the `.ds-hero--type` as one asymmetric manifesto composition: make `Make TikToks.` enormous and dominant in Razzmatazz, tuck a single cyan/pink `.ds-bubble-cluster` into the dead right side as the only secondary brand hit, and pull the current white-section thought up into the hero as a short `.ds-quote`-style kicker instead of a separate full headline. This one art-direction move would give the page a signature first screen with sharper copy concentration, stronger brand recall, and the bold editorial confidence the current stacked sections only hint at.

Do not reintroduce this already-fixed issue: The hero support copy ("creative that feels native, lands fast...") reads like ad-speak instead of sharp, casual platform language. (brand rule: avoid business jargon)

Pursue this winning direction (its fixes verifiably stuck): Rebuild `#hero` as a true `.ds-hero--type` manifesto: collapse the opening into one giant three-line `.ds-display` lockup such as "If it looks like an ad, it's gone. Make TikToks." with only "TikToks." in `#FE2C55`, scale the `.ds-bubble-cluster` up so it bleeds off the top-right edge, and tighten the CTA row directly under the type. That single art-direction move would turn the page from a solid landing page into a signature TikTok statement piece with sharper copy, clearer hierarchy, and real brand swagger.

Do not reintroduce this already-fixed issue: "See the playbook" reads like generic B2B marketing language, which undercuts the casual, creator-native voice established by the main line. (brand rule: avoid business jargon)

Do not reintroduce this already-fixed issue: The right-side bubble cluster is too small for the amount of empty black space around it, which makes the hero feel unfinished rather than intentionally bold. (brand rule: no more than one emphasis color per headline)

Do not reintroduce this already-fixed issue: The white middle section feels like a generic neutral reset between two high-energy panels; it drops the page's momentum instead of carrying the TikTok edge through. (brand rule: no grey/neutral backgrounds)
